title: Chipaktier er steget 70 pct. i år: Er AI-festen blevet en boble?
summary_md:
# Chipaktier er steget 70 pct. i år: Er AI-festen blevet en boble?

**Feed**: borsen-podcast  
**Published**: 2026-05-13  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

This Danish Børsen podcast (with strategists Henrik Henriksen and Lars Hydding) weighs a wave of solid Danish corporate earnings and a small First North juice IPO (Bardius) against the dominant market story: global chip/semiconductor stocks up ~70% this year. The hosts are constructive on quality Danish names — DSV's new AI-driven long-term targets, Demant, Lundbeck, GN Store Nord, and a re-rated Danske Bank — but openly cautious that semiconductors are a potential bubble, both saying they would not buy and would sell chip stocks now. Overall the mood is selective optimism on 'show me the money' fundamentals paired with bubble wariness on AI/chips.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 DSV — DSV | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 70%

New long-term targets plus ~6bn DKK in AI/tech savings; sharply rising EPS estimates (50→62.8→85); best-in-class M&A integrator that consistently delivers on promises.

#### Reasoning

Hosts see DSV as a quality compounder whose AI strategy de-risks the disruption fear and supports a meaningful earnings step-up. Lars holds it as a top Artescope position; the measured (non-explosive) share reaction is viewed as healthy 'show me the money' confirmation. Strong management trust and only ~6% market share leave room for further accretive acquisitions.

#### Sell Triggers

- if DSV fails to deliver on its new long-term targets
- if a value-destroying large acquisition is announced
- if AI synergy savings do not materialise

> *"DSV plejar ju att levera på det de säger att de vill levera. Så varför skulle det inte vara tillfället nu?"*

---

### 🟢 NOVO-B — Novo Nordisk | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Guidance tightened slightly — an important first 'building block' for restoring trust; Wegovy pill well received (82% of surveyed in one study prefer it); marginal newsflow improving.

#### Reasoning

After 18 months hunting for a bottom and a brutal de-rating, hosts see the trust foundation being rebuilt. Lars calls the small upgrade a critical step; Henrik holds and added at ~220 after trimming higher. Pricing pressure persists but at a decelerating pace, and the oral GLP-1 reception offers a longer-horizon catalyst.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Wegovy pill adoption disappoints
- if pricing pressure re-accelerates
- if next guidance is cut rather than held

> *"det marginale nyhetsflow begynner att bli bättre alltså pigen pejar stadigvälk ned men det är ett aftagande tempo och den här Vigovi-pillen den blir taget gott emot"*

---

### 🟡 BAVA — Bavarian Nordic | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Stuck near pre-bid levels (~189) after rejecting the 250 buyout; vaccine business doing well but unrewarded by a fragmented retail shareholder base; transition year with no CEO.

#### Reasoning

Lars is content the company stayed listed and frames it as a quiet transition year — 'no news is good news' supporting a good long-term investment. Henrik (shareholder) notes lingering trust deficit and that single-order earnings are hard to reward. Neither is adding aggressively; a wait-for-proof stance dominates.

#### Sell Triggers

- if vaccine order momentum reverses
- if no credible CEO is appointed
- if trust/communication issues persist into next results

> *"jag vill nästan säga no news is good news och så lär det bli en god langsiktig investering"*

---

### 🟢 LUN — Lundbeck | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Rewarded for guidance upgrades; positive underlying revenue/earnings trajectory; risky but high-upside CNS pipeline; rebounded after a difficult 2019-2023.

#### Reasoning

Henrik views Lundbeck as an interesting portfolio holding given continued operational improvement and the asymmetric upside from neuroscience breakthroughs. The market has rewarded its upgrades with greater conviction than Bavarian's, and the pattern of delivering after a tough multi-year stretch is one the market tends to reward.

#### Sell Triggers

- if a key pipeline asset fails in trials
- if patent/competitive pressure erodes its franchise
- if revenue growth stalls

> *"Lundbäck syns jag är spännande... det är en aktie som jag tänker är intressant att ha i portföljen"*

---

### 🟢 DEMANT — Demant | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Earnings report 'truly positive'; viewed as a super-quality company; new hearing-aid product launching well; the hearing sector starting to move.

#### Reasoning

Lars regards Demant as a super-quality name in a competitive, cyclical industry, and the new flagship product is off to a good start with investors beginning to react positively. The whole hearing-care sector showing signs of momentum strengthens the medium-term case.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the new product launch underperforms
- if the cyclical hearing market weakens
- if competitive pressure compresses margins

> *"jag betraktar Demant som en superkvalitetssällskap... deras nya stilprodukt är kommet gott i vej och investorerna begynner att reagera positivt"*

---

### 🟠 PNDORA — Pandora | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Lars added a small position during the share-price fall; company maintained its guidance and Q1/Q2 revenue, but he is not yet fully convinced.

#### Reasoning

Pandora is a consumer-driven contrast to the Vestas position Lars exited; he bought into the decline to establish a starter holding and 'get sharp' on it, but explicitly says conviction isn't there yet. The held guidance is reassuring, and the divergence vs Vestas's run-up motivates leaning toward Pandora.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Pandora cuts its maintained guidance
- if consumer demand weakens materially
- if the thesis fails to firm up on next results

> *"jag faktiskt tagit en liten bit mer i Pandora den köpte jag in under fallet sådant lite ja där man tänker nu ska jag ha en position"*

---

### 🔴 VWS — Vestas | **SELL** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Strong turnaround delivered on the market, but Lars sold his last position because earnings are harder to model/realise; rotated proceeds toward Pandora.

#### Reasoning

Despite calling Vestas a Danish icon with a fine turnaround, Lars exited because profits are difficult to 'bring home' and he sees the arrow possibly turning — admitting it may be early. The relative move (Vestas exploded up, Pandora down) drives the rotation rather than an outright bearish thesis.

#### Sell Triggers

- if order intake/profitability disappoints
- if the turnaround momentum reverses
- if wind-sector pricing pressure returns

> *"däremot så har jag sålt den sista positionen i Vestas som ju har lagt en riktigt fin turnaround... men den är lite svärare att regna hem"*

---

### 🟡 DANSKE — Danske Bank | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 55%

Re-rated to ~1.46x book after a ~53% yearly return; relaunched strategy targets higher dividends (55bn DKK 2026-2029) and buybacks, but much of the re-rating is now priced in.

#### Reasoning

Long-term constructive: strong capital-allocation plan (dividends + buybacks shrinking equity) keeps it interesting, and Jesper holds it loyally at all-time highs. But Henrik sold his bank basket in Q1 because the re-rating is largely complete short-term and these remain cyclical; he intends to re-enter later. Net: hold rather than fresh buy.

#### Sell Triggers

- if buyback/dividend plans are scaled back
- if a credit/cyclical downturn (e.g. Hormuz shock) hits earnings
- if the book-value premium proves unsustainable

> *"jag har sålt dem här i första kvartalet inte för att jag inte tror på dem... men jag syns kanske re-to-e-ratingen är skett"*

---

### 🟢 GN — GN Store Nord | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Case turned 180 degrees after selling its hearing unit (~17bn DKK proceeds); described as extremely cheap with exciting capital allocation via share buybacks.

#### Reasoning

Lars is comfortable holding GN, highlighting an extremely low valuation and an attractive buyback-driven capital-allocation story after the divestment cleaned up the case. The market hasn't yet rewarded the cash inflow — a classic 'seeing is believing' setup that could re-rate as buybacks execute.

#### Sell Triggers

- if buybacks are reduced or cancelled
- if remaining business operations deteriorate
- if proceeds are misallocated into poor M&A

> *"Och att det är något här som är knallhamrande billigt. Spännande kapitalallokering... aktie tillbakeköp"*

---

### 🟡 COLO-B — Coloplast | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Down ~11% in the portfolio; struggling to extract value from large M&A; new CEO Gavin Wood prioritising AI/digital and culture; synergy delivery is the key task.

#### Reasoning

Peter Beck's purchase is underwater and the company has yet to prove value from its multi-billion acquisitions. The new CEO leans heavily into the AI/medtech narrative and rebuilding talent appeal, but the case hinges on actually delivering synergies — a wait-and-see hold rather than a conviction buy.

#### Sell Triggers

- if M&A synergies fail to materialise under new CEO
- if margins/guidance deteriorate further
- if integration problems persist

> *"vi har tappt 11% på den in till vidare och det är ju ett av de sällskapen som verkligen inte har förmodat att få värde ut av de massiva miljarduppköp de har gjort"*

---

### 🟠 JYSK — Jyske Bank | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Neutral | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 40%

Guided to the absolute top of its range with earnings falling vs last year, disappointing some; trades cheaply (~1.03x book) but weighed by mortgage-credit exposure.

#### Reasoning

Henrik flags Jyske as guiding the very top while earnings decline, which underwhelms investors. As a cyclical bank it carries downside if macro shocks (e.g. Hormuz) hit, though the cheaper valuation versus peers keeps it on the watchlist for a potential re-entry into the bank sector.

#### Sell Triggers

- if earnings continue to fall
- if a macro/credit shock pressures the sector
- if guidance is cut from the top of the range

> *"Jyske guider toppen absolut toppen deras intervall det ramlar intjäningen för sist år och det är också något som som skuffar folk lite"*

---

### ⛔ NVDA — Nvidia | **AVOID** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 60%

Semiconductor stocks up ~70% YTD and flagged as a potential bubble; both strategists say they would not buy chip stocks and would sell any they held. Nvidia now the index's worst performer.

#### Reasoning

Hosts see semiconductors as a structurally growing but cyclically-priced sector at lofty valuations (high trailing P/E, price/sales 2-4). Both Henrik and Lars explicitly say they wouldn't buy and would sell — any exposure should be treated like a trading game with a stop-loss. A supply response or demand disappointment (as in the 1980s and 2000) could end the run within 6-12 months.

#### Sell Triggers

- if chip supply catches up to demand and pricing power fades
- if order/billings growth peaks and rolls over
- if a Taiwan/Hormuz geopolitical shock hits
- if the stock falls 20% (stop-loss)

> *"Jag vill inte köpa dem och jag vill sälja dem om jag hade dem"*

---

### 🟡 ERIC-B — Ericsson | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Allstar portfolio pick by Jesper Langmark that has delivered nearly 30% return since January; cited approvingly but without a forward directional thesis.

#### Reasoning

Ericsson is praised as a strong contributor to the podcast's model portfolio (~30% YTD), reflecting positive momentum, but the discussion is performance commentary rather than a fresh buy/sell call — implying continued holding of a winning position.

#### Sell Triggers

- if telecom-equipment demand weakens
- if the positive momentum reverses
- if guidance disappoints

> *"Ericsson som Jesper Langmark han köpte in i starten av året har ju nu leverat knappt 30 procent i avkast sedan januari"*

---
